creator: Alexander Terp
description:
`and` always returns a boolean, but `or` returns the actual operand value (short-circuit semantics). These should be consistent.

**Repro:**
```
print(1 and 2)   // true (boolean)
print(1 or 2)    // 1 (the operand value)
print(0 or 2)    // 2 (the operand value)
```

Users coming from Python/JS expect both to return operand values (short-circuit), or both to return booleans.

**Test files:** `bugs/operators/72_and_or_return_value.rad`, `78_and_return_inconsistency.rad`, `114_and_or_value_detail.rad`
title: and returns bool while or returns operand value - inconsistent
